name: Table

unions.name: Fixed
unions.comment: Provides types for fixed columns. This feature is not yet implemented.

unions.name: Msg
unions.comment:
Messages used internally by the Table module. You need to map the return from the view function to a message in your app and pass the
received sub-message to the Table module's update function. See the example including with the view function below.

aliases.name: Column
aliases.comment: The shape of a column for a particular set of data. See the examples provided within this repository for usage details.
aliases.type: { id : String, bodyCell : datum -> String, fixed : Maybe Fixed, sorting : Maybe (datum -> datum -> Order), filtering : Maybe (Filtering datum), headerCell : Maybe String, footerCell : Maybe (List datum -> String) }

aliases.name: State
aliases.comment: Holds the internal Table state. You need to hold this state in your application's model.
aliases.type: { scrollPos : Point, sorter : Dict String SortDirection, popovers : Dict String PopoverState, filters : Dict String (datum -> Bool) }

values.name: defaultColumn
values.comment: Used to quickly create a basic Column with no frills

values.name: initialState
values.comment: Creates the initial state for a Table.

values.name: update
values.comment:
Handles the messages defined above. This function must be called from your application's update function with the Table.Msg, the
original data set, the current data set (stored in your model), the current Table.State (stored in your model), and your application's
NoOp Msg.
values.type: Msg datum -> List datum -> List datum -> State datum -> msg -> Result datum msg

values.name: view
values.comment:
The entry point for the Table module. You will need to call this from your application's view function. See the examples provided
within this repository for usage details.
values.type: Config datum -> State datum -> Html (Msg datum)
